title: KPop Demon Hunters Sweeps 2026 Annie Awards
text: Netflix's animated musical KPop Demon Hunters dominated this year's Annie Awards, the animation honors presented by ASIFA-Hollywood (the L.A. Branch of the International Animated Film Association). The Sony-produced film claimed 10 prizes total. The film, directed by Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans, secured the top prize for best feature. Additional victories included accolades for direction, music, writing, and editorial work. Voice actor Arden Cho won best voice acting for her role as Rumi. The production swept technical categories, taking home awards for visual effects, character animation, character design, and production design. KPop Demon Hunters launched on Netflix last summer with minimal promotional push but became a global sensation. The streamer reported it achieved 481.6 million views worldwide during the second half of 2025, making it their most-watched film ever. The independent feature prize went to the hand-drawn French film Arco by Ugo Bienvenu. Historically, Annie Award winners frequently compete for the Academy Award's best animated feature, though last year's Annie winner Flow ultimately claimed the Oscar over the Annie Awards' choice.
